s_ch: 有原则的猎犬家系成员
t_ch: 既然这样…我只好如各位所愿，低下我高贵的头颅向各位求情了……
t_en: If that's the case... I'll just have to acquiesce to everyone's wishes, lower my esteemed head and plead with everyone...

t_ch: 不不不不不用…你站在这儿就好了，听话哈……
t_en: No no no! No need! Standing is just fine. You don't need to do that...

t_ch: （看来他还是不打算松口……）
t_en: (Looks like he still doesn't want to budge an inch...)

t_ch: 尝试再次洞察他的内心。
t_en: Try reading his inner thoughts again.

s_ch: 有原则的猎犬家系成员
t_ch: 你这个灰毛…为什么听不进我说的话？！
t_en: You punk... Why can't you understand the words that are coming out of my mouth!?

s_ch: 有原则的猎犬家系成员
t_ch: 治安官的命令就是死命令，我告诉你，前面这块地，风不能进，雨不能进，梦主来了也不能进！
t_en: The Security Officer's word is absolute law. I've told you: That place up ahead is off-limits, to wind, to rain. Even if the Dreammaster was here, they aren't getting in!

t_ch: 「黄金的时刻」又不下雨。
t_en: It doesn't rain in the Golden Hour.

s_ch: 有原则的猎犬家系成员
t_ch: 这只是个比喻，比喻！懂吗？！
t_en: That's just a metaphor. A metaphor! Don't you get it!?

t_ch: 梦主来了也不能进吗？
t_en: Even the Dreammaster isn't getting in?

s_ch: 有原则的猎犬家系成员
t_ch: 废话！梦主来了也不能……
t_en: Did you not hear me? Even if the Dreammaster was here...

s_ch: 有原则的猎犬家系成员
t_ch: …呃，梦主来了再说！而且，这只是个比喻，比喻！懂吗？！
t_en: ...Uhh, we'll talk about this if the Dreammaster is ever here! And, it's just a metaphor. A metaphor! Don't you get it!?

s_ch: 有原则的猎犬家系成员
t_ch: 我警告你啊，你再这样不依不饶，我可要展现真正的实力了！
t_en: I'm warning you, if you keep insisting, I'm gonna have to show you my true power!

t_ch: 那个，{NICKNAME}，他好像有点生气了，咱要不还是……
t_en: Uhm, {NICKNAME}, he looks kinda angry now. Should we...

t_ch: 来就来，谁怕谁！
t_en: Come at me, bro!

s_ch: 有原则的猎犬家系成员
t_ch: 唉，人类总是重复同样的错误……
t_en: Humans never learn...

t_ch: 你敢违抗拥有球棒的我吗？
t_en: You dare defy me and my baseball bat?

s_ch: 有原则的猎犬家系成员
t_ch: 唉，人类总是无法互相理解的……
t_en: Humans will never understand one another...

t_ch: …得，这下克里珀来了也存护不了他了。
t_en: ...Great, even if Qlipoth were here, THEY wouldn't be able to protect him.

s_ch: 有原则的猎犬家系成员
t_ch: 抵抗只会白白送死，为什么就是不明白——上吧，美梦剧团！给我狠狠地教训{M#他们}{F#她们}！
t_en: There is no meaning to death in defiance, why do you not understand... Sweet Dreams Troupe, charge! Teach them a lesson they won't forget!

s_ch: 有原则的猎犬家系成员
t_ch: 又要重复这样的结果了吗…看来你我都陷入这绝望的轮回里了啊。
t_en: Am I destined to relive this outcome...? Looks like you and I have been plunged into a cycle of despair.

t_ch: …他在说什么？
t_en: ...What's he talking about?

s_ch: 有原则的猎犬家系成员
t_ch: 但是，这一次，我会斩断这悲剧的锁链——你们无论如何都不能过去，除非跨过我的尸体！
t_en: However, this time, I will sever the chains of tragedy — the only way any of you will pass, is over my dead body!

t_ch: 这大哥心态也太沉重了…吧。
t_en: He doesn't have to be so melodramatic, does he?

t_ch: （看来他还是不打算松口……）
t_en: (Looks like he still doesn't want to budge an inch...)

t_ch: 尝试再次洞察他的内心。
t_en: Try reading his inner thoughts again.

t_ch: 我说怎么这么吵…是你们啊，欢迎。都找到这来了，有什么事？
t_en: I was wondering what all the commotion was... Oh, it's you guys. Welcome. Since you made it here, what can I do for you?

t_ch: 加拉赫先生，您好。听您的语气，您知道我们会来这里？
t_en: Hello, Mr. Gallagher, sir. Judging from your tone, it sounds like you were expecting us.

t_ch: 哈哈哈…姬子小姐太客气了，对我不必用「您」。
t_en: Hahaha... Miss Himeko, you're too polite. There's no need to call me "Sir."

t_ch: …加拉赫先生还知道我的名字？
t_en: ...Mr. Gallagher, you even know my name?

t_ch: 知道，怎么不知道？你们可是大名鼎鼎的星穹列车，「钟表匠」的贵客。
t_en: Of course I do! You folks are from the legendary Astral Express, and honorable guests of the Watchmaker.

t_ch: 我在黄金的时刻与这位{M#先生}{F#小姐}有过一面之缘，记得当时那位银色头发的小姑娘也在……
t_en: I had an encounter with this {M#gentleman}{F#lady} in the Golden Hour. I remember that little silver-haired girl was there too...

t_ch: …我对那孩子的遭遇深表惋惜。
t_en: ...I'm sorry for what happened to that kid.

t_ch: 我们奉家族之命来调查此事。
t_en: That's exactly what The Family has ordered us to investigate.

t_ch: 我们也很遗憾…
t_en: We are sorry too...

t_ch: 这也是我们前来拜访加拉赫先生的理由。列车不能对那孩子的死坐视不理，决定协助家族查清真相，希望能为她讨个公道。
t_en: This is also the reason why we've come to visit you, Mr. Gallagher. The Express can't just overlook the death of that child, so we've decided to help The Family get to the bottom of it, in the hopes of getting justice for her.

t_ch: 无名客竟也和家族搅和在一起了…天意弄人啊。
t_en: The Nameless involved with The Family... What an unpredictable twist of fate.

t_ch: 家族…怎么了吗？
t_en: Why...? What's wrong with The Family?

t_ch: 没什么，别在意。在匹诺康尼，所有人都喜欢家族。
t_en: It's nothing. On Penacony, everyone loves The Family.

t_ch: 再怎么抗拒「美梦」的人，到了时候，也会变得舍不得。有谁会愿意离开温暖的窝？只有傻瓜、小孩子…还有脑袋不清醒的酒鬼。
t_en: No matter how much one resists the "beautiful dream," when the time comes, they too will find it hard to let go... Who wants to leave a warm nest? Just idiots, little kids... and inebriated fools.

t_ch: …加拉赫先生似乎意有所指。
t_en: Mr. Gallagher seems to be getting at something.

t_ch: 你误会了，我没有。
t_en: You got it wrong, I'm not.

t_ch: 你们想聊案子？可以，跟我来吧，这地不适合说话，咱们挪个窝。
t_en: You want to discuss the case? Sure, come with me. This is not a good place to talk. Let's go elsewhere.

t_ch: 即便发生了那样耸人听闻的惨案，这片美梦也依旧在有条不紊地运作啊。
t_en: Even after that chilling tragedy, this dream is still running effortlessly.

t_ch: 除了「同谐」的家族，很难想象宇宙中还有哪一方势力，能维系一座如此庞大的建筑。
t_en: Other than The Family of the Harmony, it's hard to imagine any other power in the universe that could sustain a building of such magnitude.

t_ch: 家族本身也是一座巨大、完美的建筑，就像…一尊活着的神像。
t_en: The Family itself is a huge, perfect building. Like... A living idol.

t_ch: 每位家族成员都将自己视作神体的一块拼图，围绕着唯一的{RUBY_B#星神}核心{RUBY_E#}、共同的{RUBY_B#同谐}理想{RUBY_E#}，在祂的指挥下，忠诚地各司其职，奉献自我，同时又反受其给养。
t_en: Each member of The Family sees themselves as a piece of the divine puzzle, revolving around a singular {RUBY_B#Aeon}core{RUBY_E#} and {RUBY_B#Harmony}a shared ideal{RUBY_E#}. Under THEIR command, they loyally fulfill their roles, offering themselves while also receiving sustenance in return.

t_ch: 很有趣的比喻，或许这就是匹诺康尼的「美梦」得以长存的根本。
t_en: Interesting analogy. Perhaps that's why Penacony's "beautiful dream" has persisted for so long.

t_ch: 但人体终有其时，神躯亦然。
t_en: But the human body has its limits, and so does the divine's.

t_ch: 这就不像是一位「巡海游侠」会发表的评论了。
t_en: That doesn't sound like the kind of comment a Galaxy Ranger would make.

t_ch: 只是点出事实，瓦尔特先生一定比我更能参透个中滋味。
t_en: Just pointing out the facts. Mr. Yang will definitely have a better sense of what's going on than I do.

t_ch: 黄泉小姐何出此言？
t_en: Why do you say that, Miss Acheron?

t_ch: 美梦正在崩溃，但并不因为某柱星神、某个派系，或某位具体的来客。它的崩溃源自某种人性的必然，家族不愿承认这点，却在无形中反成了催化剂……
t_en: The beautiful dream is crumbling, but not because of a particular Aeon, a particular faction, or a particular visitor. Its collapse stems from a certain inevitability of human nature. The Family refuses to acknowledge this, and it has ultimately backfired and become a catalyst...

t_ch: 当人放任精神沉溺于无需代价，没有痛苦，只有安逸和享乐的梦境时，他们和「坏死」的距离便会越来越近。无论他认为自己活在何种极乐中，死亡都是无从改变的结局。
t_en: As people immerse their souls in the Dreamscape, where consequences and pain cease to exist, and only ease and pleasure prevail, they draw closer and closer to necrosis. Regardless of the perceived bliss, death looms as the inevitable conclusion.

t_ch: 并且，这种坏死会传播、扩散，一块拼图的异变最终会导致整座建筑的摇晃、破碎…崩坏。
t_en: Also, this necrosis will diffuse and spread. One piece of the puzzle's mutation will eventually cause the entire building to shake, break... and crumble.

t_ch: 最后人们为自由而建的美梦，会反过来成为囚禁自我的牢笼。
t_en: ...In the end, the dreams that people built in the name of freedom became the cage that imprisoned them.

t_ch: 想必黄泉小姐此行收获不小，愿意同我分享一下吗？
t_en: I'm sure you've gained a lot from this trip, Miss Acheron. Are you willing to share your findings with me?

t_ch: 当然…前提是我还记得。
t_en: Of course... That's if I remember.

t_ch: 在这样说时，她的手也轻轻搭在了刀镡上——
t_en: She says this as her hand gently rests on the hilt of the sword.

t_ch: ——又很快放下，转瞬即逝。
t_en: — And then quickly lets go, in the blink of an eye.

t_ch: 别在意，只是习惯。因为一些过往，我变得很容易…遗忘，只有当这柄刀出鞘时，那些朦胧的景象才会逐渐清晰。
t_en: Don't mind me. It's just a habit. Owing to events in the past, I've become easily... forgetful. It's only when this sword is unsheathed that those hazy memories start to become clearer.

t_ch: 请随意。
t_en: Take your time.

t_ch: 足够了，在匹诺康尼发生的事我记得很清楚。请问吧。
t_en: That should do it. I vividly remember everything that occurred on Penacony. Ask away.

t_ch: 关于「黎明的时刻」…
t_en: Regarding the Moment of Daybreak...

t_ch: 黎明的时刻——我听说那里坐落着加工梦境基底的「早霞工厂」。
t_en: The Moment of Daybreak — I've heard that's where the "Dawn Factory," which processes the foundation of the Dreamscape, is located.

t_ch: 梦境的声色犬马背后，是一座座「想象」的工厂。工人们日复一日地在梦中创造各种奇思妙想的商品，然后回到现实中在与豪华客房相去甚远的卧榻上休息。
t_en: Behind the Dreamscape's song and dance, stand many "imagination" factories. Workers create all kinds of whimsical works day in and day out in their dreams, then they return to reality and sleep on a narrow bed in a room — a far cry from luxury.

t_ch: 他们说这就足够，光怪陆离的梦境已是最好的报酬。
t_en: They say it will suffice. Experiencing the bizarre and motley Dreamscape is the best reward.

t_ch: 我在那里遇见一位少女，她刚成年，正是应该纵情享受美梦的年纪。她最大的愿望是有朝一日能迁至黄金的时刻，看看由自己的双手织就的华服。
t_en: There, I encountered a young woman who had just come of age, the perfect time to indulge in beautiful dreams. Her greatest wish was to one day move to the Golden Hour and see the magnificent garments that she had woven with her own hands.

t_ch: 由于某些原因，她的愿望很难实现。不过我想办法带了一件衣裳给她。
t_en: For certain reasons, her wish was difficult to fulfill. But I managed to bring her a garment.

t_ch: 关于「烫金的时刻」…
t_en: Regarding the Gilded Hour...

t_ch: 烫金的时刻…据说那里是匹诺康尼的货币中心。
t_en: Gilded Hour... It's said to be Penacony's Currency Center.

t_ch: 嗯…那是一座森严得如同要塞的金融之城，梦境的经济心脏。苜蓿草家系的皮皮西们在那里维持它的运转，将纸钞构成的血液送向巨人匹诺康尼的各处。
t_en: Yes... It is a fortress-like financial city, the economic heart of the Dreamscape. The Pepeshi people of the Alfalfa Family are there to keep it running, sending blood that is made from money everywhere on Penacony.

t_ch: 那里所有人都衣着精致，脚步匆匆。进入「烫金的时刻」工作，是当地皮皮西人对后代最大的愿望。
t_en: Everyone there is exquisitely dressed and always in a hurry. The greatest wish of the local Pepeshi people is for their future generations to work in the Gilded Hour.

t_ch: 我没有遇见任何愿意交谈的人。只能站在十字路口，看着一群又一群人像风一样匆匆掠过钢铁的丛林，只为把赚来的苜蓿币锁进银行金库。
t_en: I've never met anyone who was willing to talk. I could only stand at the crossroads, watching crowds of people hurrying like the wind through the jungle of steel, only to deposit the Alfalfa credits that they'd earned into the bank's vault.

t_ch: 我不知道他们是否还会打开那金库的门。但在我离开前，我亲眼目睹一位衣冠楚楚的皮皮西从空中跌落，而周围的人依旧穿过了他。
t_en: I don't know if they would open the vault door, but before I left, I witnessed a well-dressed Pepeshi person plummet from the sky while those around him continued on their way, unfazed.

t_ch: 关于「蓝调的时刻」…
t_en: About the Blue Hour...

t_ch: 听说蓝调的时刻十分浪漫，黄泉小姐可曾见证了什么故事？
t_en: I hear the Blue Hour is very romantic. Do you have any tales to share?

t_ch: 也许瓦尔特先生也听说过，那里有一艘名为「黄昏号」的巨轮停泊在梦海之上，轻歌曼舞，夜夜不休。
t_en: Perhaps Mr. Yang has heard — there is a large boat called the "Eventide" anchored along the Sea of Dreams, where soft music and dancing persist endlessly every night.

t_ch: 我在那里遇到一位苍老的妇人，她在港口盼着多年前离开的爱人归来，在停滞的时间里等待了无数的时间。
t_en: I ran into a wizened lady there. She was at the dock waiting for her long-departed lover to return, waiting for countless hours within time that stood still.

t_ch: 潮湿的海风里，她谈起自己的青春——就像许多渴望财富与机遇的人们，他们为了追逐梦想来到匹诺康尼，他的意识却消失在了梦海深处。
t_en: In the humid sea breeze, she spoke of her own youth — like many who desired wealth and love, they came to Penacony to pursue their dreams. Alas, her lover's consciousness was lost in the dark depths of the Sea of Dreams.

t_ch: 末了，她提议坐上舢板，在近海上继续我们的对谈。我答应了，同她一起登上小船，可她却再没说些什么，只是茫然地望了海天相接的尽头许久。
t_en: Finally, she suggested we continue our conversation on a boat in the shallows. I agreed, and boarded the boat with her, but she never said anything, her eyes absent-mindedly gazing at the horizon for what seemed like forever.

t_ch: 最后，我们退回了沙滩。
t_en: Finally, we retreated to the beach.

t_ch: 关于「薄暮的时刻」…
t_en: About the Moment of Dusk...

t_ch: 时尚、奢侈与消费主义的梦境，薄暮的时刻，我的同伴也拜访了那里。
t_en: The Dreamscape of chic, luxury and consumerism — the Moment of Dusk — my companions have been there too.

t_ch: 那你们一定见识过为实现梦想、或已然实现梦想的人们在那里挥金如土、孤注一掷。一切皆可标价，一切皆可买卖——哪怕是梦想本身。
t_en: Then you all must have seen those who are attempting to realize their dreams, or have realized them, scattering money as if it were dust, and betting it on all or nothing. Everything has a price, and everything can be bought or sold — even dreams themselves.

t_ch: 我在那里看见一位智械，他准备的拍品是「自我」。一旦有人竞拍成功，在约定的期限和规则下，他会践行买家的一切指示，成为那人绝对的所有物。
t_en: I saw an Intellitron there who was preparing to auction himself. When someone wins a bid, under stipulated periods and rules, he would do the buyer's every bidding, becoming that person's very possession.

t_ch: 那智械一共被拍卖了十二次，我参加了他的第十三次拍卖会。那是我见过最人声鼎沸的盛宴，但再也没人将目光投向他。
t_en: That Intellitron had been auctioned off a dozen times, and I participated in his thirteenth. That was the grandest banquet I had ever attended, but never again did anyone cast another glance at him.

t_ch: 这一次，他流拍了。
t_en: This time around, there were no successful bids for him.

t_ch: 就这些吧。
t_en: That's everything.

t_ch: 这便是我这一路浅浅的见闻……
t_en: This is what I've seen and heard along the way...

t_ch: 曾有人这么对我说：匹诺康尼在很久以前并非如此，匹诺康尼也不应如此。
t_en: Someone once said to me: Penacony wasn't like this a long time ago, nor should it be.

t_ch: 我一路走过盛会之星的现实和梦境，看着黑夜升起又落下，时光为人们停驻，而精神的富有和贫穷…也永远停留在各自的刻度。
t_en: I've traveled through the reality and Dreamscape of the Planet of Festivities, watched the tides of night rise and fall, when time stopped for people. Where the spirits of the rich and impoverished... remain forever fixed on their own scales.

t_ch: 所以我认为「美梦」的崩溃是必然。
t_en: This is why I think the collapse of the beautiful dream is inevitable.

t_ch: 也许有办法改变这一切。
t_en: There might be a way to change everything.

t_ch: 也许吧。但如果这正是人们所期望的世界——<color=#ff3a3e>如果这正是生命选择沉睡的原因</color>——我们还应令它做出改变吗？
t_en: Perhaps. But if this is indeed the world that people desire — <color=#ff3a3e>if this is precisely why life chooses to slumber</color> — should we still seek to change it?

t_ch: …黄泉小姐，换我来为你分享一个故事吧。
t_en: ...Miss Acheron, now it's my turn to share a story with you.

t_ch: 在我的故乡有一个男人，在世界面临难以愈合的伤痛之际，他做出了一个决定。
t_en: There was a man from my homeland who, at a time when the world was grappling with deep, unhealable pain, made a choice.

t_ch: 他将世界上所有人的梦编织在一起，将人与人的梦境彼此连缀，再以己身背负，他由此创造出一名巨人，一位「精神的亚当」。
t_en: He wove together the dreams of everyone in the world, linking people's dreamscapes, and shouldered this burden himself. From this, he created a giant, a "Spiritual Adam."

t_ch: 从此，那巨人立于天地之间，成为整个世界存续的支柱。而作为代价，那些难以前进，无法前进的人…他们将永远失去「未来」。
t_en: Since that moment, the giant stood between heaven and earth, becoming the pillar of the world's existence. As a price, those who found it hard to move forward, who could not advance... forever lost their "future."

t_ch: 他们沉眠于没有灾难和苦痛的梦里，在男人创造的理想乡中度过安然一生。而「精神的亚当」会因这些人不愿醒来的愿望…变得坚不可摧。
t_en: They slumbered in a dream devoid of disaster and pain, living out their days peacefully in the man's created utopia. And it is because of the wishes of those people, who wished not to awaken, that this Spiritual Adam became unbreakable.

t_ch: 但如今你却站在此处，这也就代表…那个男人失败了。
t_en: And yet, you stand here right now, which also means... that man failed.

t_ch: 因为人们总要走向未来。纵使人性的弱点让他们驻足停步，但在真正无法前行的时候…人类一定会试图拯救自己。
t_en: Because people must always move towards the future. Even if human weaknesses make them pause, when they truly cannot move forward... humanity will eventually seek a way to save itself.

t_ch: 而那个男人…他也从来不是失败者。他与那世界的每个人一样，将人性的可能铭记于心。
t_en: And that man... He was NEVER a failure. Like everyone in that world, he etched the possibilities of human nature into his heart.

t_ch: 他是神话中的{RUBY_B#伊卡洛斯}逐日者{RUBY_E#}，向天飞翔，并以坠落迎来自己最终的胜利。
t_en: He was the {RUBY_B#Icarus}sunchaser{RUBY_E#} of legend, soaring towards the sky, and embracing his final victory with his fall.

t_ch: 他高高升起，只为来到太阳面前，那是没有任何人曾经到达的地方。他将因之融化，陨落大海，而在那之后……
t_en: He ascended to heights uncharted, only to come face to face with the sun — a place not visited by anyone before. His wings would melt because of it, only for him to fall into the sea, and after that...

t_ch: …将有无数的人越过他的身躯，飞上更高的天际。
t_en: ...countless others would surpass him, soaring to even greater heights.

t_ch: …很符合无名客的「开拓」精神。
t_en: ...A fitting metaphor for the Nameless' Trailblazing spirit.

t_ch: 谢谢，瓦尔特先生。我知道你想确认什么。宇宙中有着无数相似却又相异的世界。在这些世界中，也有无数相似却又相异的人。
t_en: Thank you, Mr. Yang. I know what you wish to confirm. The universe has innumerable similar, yet different worlds. In these worlds, there are innumerable people who look alike, yet don't.

t_ch: 我也曾踏上旅途，在不同的世界邂逅容貌相似的「故人」，目睹他们的命运行过似曾相识的轨迹。所以，我会告诉你……
t_en: I, too have embarked on journeys, encountering "old friends" with familiar faces on different worlds, witnessing their destinies follow paths similar to mine. So, I will tell you...

t_ch: 尽管不完全相同，但你所描绘的这个故事…它和我的过往重叠在一起。而在那深不见底的梦中……
t_en: Even if not completely similar, this story you just told... It overlaps with my past. And within that abyssal dream...

t_ch: …我结束了那个男人的生命，独自一人。
t_en: ...I ended that man's life, alone.

t_ch: 我不是你认识的那个人，我的故乡也未能像你们的世界那样幸运。
t_en: I am not who you think I am, nor will my home be as fortunate as your world.

t_ch: …我很遗憾。
t_en: ...I am sorry.

t_ch: 没关系，如果这能消解你的疑虑，我不介意。
t_en: It's fine. I don't mind, so long as I can alleviate your suspicions.

t_ch: 但我仍想知道，在那「巡猎」的表象下，黄泉小姐，究竟是*哪一种力量*驱使着你独行至今。
t_en: ...There's something I still wish to know, Miss Acheron. Under that representation of The Hunt, exactly what sort of power is it that has motivated your solitary journey thus far?

t_ch: …瓦尔特先生，在回答这个问题前，我想先继续刚才的话题。
t_en: ...Mr. Yang, before answering that question, I wish to continue the previous topic.

t_ch: 我很喜欢你的比喻。诚然，鸟儿生来就会飞翔，但在遥远的曾经，它们的祖先也只得从地面仰望高天。
t_en: I like your analogy very much. Indeed, birds are born to fly, but in a distant past, their ancestors could only gaze at the sky in envy.

t_ch: 它们看见那遥远的，来自天外的光芒洞穿云翳，普照大地。于是一次又一次，一代又一代，鸟儿们展翅高翔，试图触碰天顶，只因太阳就在那里。
t_en: They saw that faraway ray of light from above the heavens, piercing through the clouds and blanketing the earth. And so, time and time again, generation after generation, the birds spread their wings and took to the sky, attempting to touch its ceiling — all because the sun was there.

t_ch: 那么，如果当最后的鸟儿终于飞上天际，却看见光芒的尽头并非太阳，而是{RUBY_B#吞噬一切的黑洞}漆黑的大日{RUBY_E#}……
t_en: Then, what if the last bird finally soars into the sky, only to realize that the end of the light is not the sun, but {RUBY_B#All-Consuming Black Hole}darkness{RUBY_E#}...

t_ch: 那我们究竟是为了什么…才要向光而行？
t_en: Then why exactly... do we even walk towards the light?

t_ch: 好久不见啊！在匹诺康尼玩得还开心吗——「黄泉」？
t_en: Long time no see! Having fun on Penacony, Acheron?

t_ch: （这声音…不是先前那位康士坦丝。是她的同伴？）
t_en: (This voice... It's not Constance. Could it be her companion?)

t_ch: 虽然不知道你究竟是什么东西，又在打什么主意……
t_en: Though I don't know exactly what you are or what you're up to...

t_ch: 但我的子弹马上就会找到你了——在那之前，你最好赶紧在匹诺康尼找个棺材铺，让老板留一副质量好的给自己，冒牌货。
t_en: My bullets will find you — until then, you best find a casket store on Penacony, and ask the owner to reserve a good quality casket for you, imposter!

t_ch: （「冒牌货」？原来如此，她把我的行踪带给了另一个在追踪黄泉的人。）
t_en: (Imposter? I see. She gave my whereabouts to someone who is tracking Acheron too.)

t_ch: 你是谁？
t_en: Who are you?

t_ch: 嗯？我打错了？宝了个贝的，你又是谁？
t_en: Huh? Did I make a mistake? Who the fudge are you?

t_ch: 我是流光忆庭的忆者。
t_en: I'm the Garden of Recollection's Memokeeper.

t_ch: 嚯！不错，我就喜欢这种硬茬。你是那个冒牌货的保镖？还是别的什么人？算了，无所谓。我也会留一发子弹给你的，洗干净脑门等着吧。
t_en: Heh! Not bad. This is the kind of tough challenge I like. Are you that imposter's bodyguard? Never mind. It's fine. I'll leave a round for you, so get that forehead clean and wait for me!

t_ch: 我不知道你在说什么——但你认识黄泉，那个巡海游侠，对么？我有事要问你。
t_en: I don't know what you're talking about — but you know Acheron, the Galaxy Ranger, yes? I have something to ask you.

t_ch: 哈哈，要我帮你写遗嘱？可以，你说吧。
t_en: Haha, are you asking me to write your will? Sure, go ahead.

t_ch: 不是什么遗嘱——我只想问你，她究竟是如何*变成*巡海游侠的。
t_en: Not quite. I only want to ask, how exactly did she become a Galaxy Ranger?

t_ch: 她根本不是「巡猎」的命途行者，你才是，对么？告诉我，黄泉究竟是什么来头。
t_en: She's clearly not a Pathstrider of The Hunt. But you are, aren't you? Tell me, what's Acheron's deal?

t_ch: …哈哈哈哈哈，可以！没想到是友军，他宝贝了个腿的，看来我真是撞大运了。
t_en: Hahahaha, sure! Never thought I'd come across an ally. Fudge, what a stroke of luck!

t_ch: 我马上就到匹诺康尼了，忆者，去买瓶「阿斯德纳白橡木」，温好，敬你一杯。
t_en: I'll be on Penacony soon. Memokeeper, go buy a bottle of Asdana's White Oak and warm it up, and I'll raise a glass to you.

t_ch: 那女人的过去？没人知道。但如果你要的只是个简单的答案，可以，你最好找张椅子垫在下面，那个叫黄泉的女人——
t_en: That lady's past? Nobody knows. But if all you want is a simple answer, sure, you best get a chair and take a seat. That woman named Acheron...

t_ch: ——是个*不该存在*的令使。
t_en: —is an Emanator who should not exist.

t_ch: 「…星际和平公司宣布，战略投资部已全面完成对察夸卡德星链的回收及整治工作——」
t_en: The IPC has announced that its Strategic Investment Department has completed the recovery and remediation of the Chakwakad Chain...

t_ch: 「察夸卡德星链地处阿库讷瓦卡德星区，曾是公司重要的贸易埠头，但在星际能源战争期间，遭到费兹杰罗准军事海军联合武力窃夺——」
t_en: The Chakwakad Chain, located in the Acunawakad Sector, was once a vital trading port for the IPC. However, it was captured by the combined forces of the Fitzgerald Paramilitary Navy during the Interstellar Energy War...

t_ch: 「此后该地区逐步沦为『泛星系奴隶市场』的枢纽，大量星际海盗亦将此地据为己有，对临近星区贸易航运造成极大的威胁——」
t_en: What was left of the sector became a hub for the Interstellar Slave Market and has fallen into the hands of space pirates, posing a significant threat to trade and shipping in the neighboring sectors...

t_ch: 「在战略投资部员工『翡翠』的指导下，星链现已重归存护的版图，但泛星系奴隶贸易和海盗活动的泛滥，仍是公司迫切关注的问题。公司将不遗余力……」
t_en: Under the guidance of Jade, an esteemed member of the Strategic Investment Department, the Star Chain has now been returned to its rightful owner. However, the rampant spread of the Interstellar Slave Trade and piracy remains a pressing concern for the IPC. The IPC is fully committed to...

t_ch: 回来了啊，<unbreak>35</unbreak>号，喜欢你的护身符么？
t_en: So, No. <unbreak>35</unbreak>, you're back. Like your new lucky charm?

t_ch: …「商品编码」也能当做护身符么？
t_en: ...Can a "commodity code" really be considered a lucky charm?

t_ch: 闭嘴。我可没允许你说话，茨冈尼亚的鬣狗。
t_en: Silence. I didn't give you permission to speak, you Sigonian hound.

t_ch: 那群穿黑西装的没讲太多，所以我不知道你到底用了什么手段，才在当年那场大屠杀里保住了小命。
t_en: The guys in black didn't say much, so I've no idea what you did to save your skin in that massacre back in the day.

t_ch: 但我认为你很幸运，就把你买下了。从今往后，你和你的运气都是我的资产，明白了么？
t_en: But I figured you must have had good luck, so I bought you. From now on, you and your good luck are MY assets. Are we clear?

t_ch: 给你的第一个命令很简单：除你以外，我还买了另外三十…嗯，三十四个奴隶。
t_en: Your first task is simple. In addition to you, I've purchased thirty... well, thirty-four other slaves.

t_ch: 去跟他们玩场「游戏」——两天时间，活着出来，证明你的本事货真价实。
t_en: Go and play a "game" with them. If you come out alive after two days, it proves that you're the real deal.

t_ch: …你疯了。
t_en: ...You're insane.

t_ch: 呵，验验货罢了。
t_en: Heh, testing out if you're a good product.

t_ch: 你就不怕这钱白花了？
t_en: Aren't you worried that the money you spent on me will go to waste?

t_ch: 老子有的是钱，小金毛。泛星系奴隶市场最不缺你这种自以为是的小屁孩。
t_en: I've got stacks on stacks, blondie. The slave market is never short of self-righteous brats like you.

t_ch: 但你有副不错的皮囊，所以不少客人都把身家押在你这瘦骨嶙峋的小鬼身上。去吧，别让主子失望。
t_en: But you look good, and that's why many customers are betting their fortunes on a scrawny brat like you. So, go along now, and don't let your master down.

t_ch: …你花了多少？
t_en: ...How much did you spend?

t_ch: 什么？
t_en: What?

t_ch: 我的价格，你花了多少钱买我？
t_en: My price. How much did you pay for me?

t_ch: 嚯，想知道这个？可以。六十枚{RUBY_B#赤铜币}塔安巴{RUBY_E#}，不多不少。
t_en: Huh, you really want to know? Well, it was sixty {RUBY_B#Red Copper Coins}Tanba{RUBY_E#}. No more, no less.

t_ch: …我要和你赌。
t_en: ...I'll take my chances.

t_ch: 六十的一半，三十个子儿…只要我能活着回来，你就得给我。你敢赌么？
t_en: Thirty Tanba... If I come back alive, you'll give me thirty Tanba. Deal?

t_ch: 哈哈，想跟我赌？可以，你有种！
t_en: Hahaha, are you trying to strike a bet with me? Well, you've got some guts!

t_ch: ——但抱歉，不可能。奴隶，别忘了自己的身份，你压根没有上桌的资格。
t_en: Sorry, but that won't do. Don't forget your place, slave. You're not qualified to be at the table.

t_ch: 你就是一枚筹码，被别人捏在手里丢出去的命，要么就帮主人带着更多筹码回来，要么…就再也别回来。
t_en: You're just a chip, a life thrown away in someone else's hands. Either you come back with more chips for your master, or... you never come back.

t_ch: 「所有，或一无所有」——千万别让我丢脸啊，*幸运儿*。
t_en: It's all or nothing. Don't embarrass me, my lucky hound.

t_ch: 脸色很差啊。还是说，这也是你的演技？
t_en: You look pale. Or, is that also part of your act?

t_ch: 我没想到你还有脸来见我。
t_en: Didn't think you'd have the nerve to show yourself.

t_ch: 我以为这才是你想要的结果，毕竟我可是像你说的那样——「忠实地履行了自己的职责」。
t_en: I thought this was exactly what you wanted. After all, I faithfully fulfilled my duties as you instructed.

t_ch: 你要是挺不住了，记得先通知我一声。
t_en: Just tell me if you can't hold on any longer.

t_ch: 庸众院的「天才」是打算替我收尸？天啊…真是荣幸。
t_en: So, the "genius" of the Council of Mundanites wants to be my undertaker now? My... what an honor.

t_ch: 战略投资部的各位一定也非常希望能及时收到你的死讯。别忘了，你再也没法见到他们了，这个任务现在可是落在我的头上。
t_en: Yeah, and I'm pretty sure the people at the Strategic Investment Department would love to be notified of your death in due time, but let's not forget you won't be seeing them, because I'm the manager of this task.

t_ch: 好啊，那麻烦你现在就去报个信吧。就说「砂金已经做好万全准备，十七个系统时后即可入场。」
t_en: Great, then tell your people that Aventurine is ready to go in seventeen system hours.

t_ch: 大言不惭。你打算怎么在被「同谐」禁锢的情况下完成任务？
t_en: You've got a lot of nerve — how exactly do you plan on completing your task while your hands are tied by the Harmony?

t_ch: 和星期日的对谈让我确信了家族中有叛徒，而匹诺康尼的秘密就在那人手中…借此机会，我也把所有基石放在了它们该在的位置上。
t_en: My conversation with Sunday convinced me that there's a traitor in The Family, and that they hold the secrets of Penacony... So, I took the opportunity to set everything in motion.

t_ch: 而现在我还成功拿回了礼金，自打踏入白日梦酒店的大门，事情就没像这样顺利过…看着吧，距离胜利我只差一步之遥了。
t_en: I even managed to recover the gift money. Things haven't gone this smoothly since I walked through the doors of The Reverie... Now, I'm only one step away from victory. Let's just wait and see.

t_ch: 听起来你只是把自己的惨状复述了一遍，用的还是极其嘴硬的方式。
t_en: Sounds like a very elaborate way of saying that you failed.

t_ch: 我能说的就这些。忘了吗？你已经背叛过我了，教授。
t_en: That's all I can say. Have you forgotten, Doctor? You betrayed me.

t_ch: 去你该去的地方吧，我迫不及待想看到公司舰队包围匹诺康尼的样子了。你也拿到你想要的东西了，不是么？
t_en: Go, do what you must. I look forward to the sight of the IPC fleet surrounding Penacony. You've achieved what you desired, haven't you?

t_ch: 确实，但怎么着？你那袋礼金里，还藏了呼叫近地轨道支援的信标不成？
t_en: That's true, but what's your plan? Did you conceal an Orbital Support beacon in that gift money bag?

t_ch: 搞不好呢？兴许这就是我死到临头还想着发钱的原因。
t_en: Well, who knows? Maybe that's why I'm handing out cash even when I'm about to bite the dust.

t_ch: …你彻底疯了，该死的赌徒。
t_en: ...You are indeed a gambler — an insane one at that.

t_ch: 也许我早疯了，谁知道呢？
t_en: Well, maybe I am. Who knows?

t_ch: 算了。给你这个，拿着。死到临头再打开它，你会感谢我的。
t_en: Fine. Here, take this. Open it when you're on your last legs. You'll thank me.

t_ch: 这什么玩意…医嘱？
t_en: What's this...? Medical advice?

t_ch: …呵，你是懂戏剧性的，教授。
t_en: You catch on pretty fast, Doctor.

t_ch: 要我探案，又不给半点线索…真有你的，脑袋长翅膀的混蛋。
t_en: Asking me to solve a case without giving a single clue... How typical of you, you wing-headed scoundrel.

t_ch: 但你们为那个偷渡犯如坐针毡的样子，倒是应了我的*猜想*。接下来…就让公司的财富之雨平等地落在每个人头上吧。
t_en: But the way you're all on edge about that stowaway, it's just as I guessed it would be. As for now... let the rain of wealth from the IPC fall evenly on everyone.

t_ch: 唉…世风日下，人心不古……
t_en: The world has truly lost its way...

t_ch: 这些宝石送给你。
t_en: Here, I got these gems for you.

t_ch: 你…哦，我懂了，这一定是什么整蛊节目吧！你肯定在那边的楼上架了摄影机，好拍下自己行善的过程，是不是？
t_en: You... Wait, I get it now! This is some sort of prank show, right? You must have some cameras set up around here to film yourself doing good deeds, right?

t_ch: 你们这些年轻人，一天到晚净想着剑走偏锋，这可不行。真正的好戏是要用心才能拍出来的。
t_en: You youngsters are always looking for a quick way to get an audience, but you know what? A truly great show never comes easy.

t_ch: 好戏很快就会上演了，老人家。但在那之前，我想向您打听件事。您知道这梦里哪儿能找到「死亡」吗？
t_en: A great show will start soon, old man. But before that, I need to ask you something. Do you know where I can find "Death" in this dream?

t_ch: 哦！又是个「不怕死」的年轻人。给你句忠告，别觉得自己的想法有多么超前。
t_en: Ah, I see! Another fearless youngster looking for death, huh? Well, let me give you a piece of advice. Don't think you're the first one who's ever thought of that idea.

t_ch: 死亡？这类题材早烂大街了，我还在爱德华医生那儿买到过呢。说是什么…只此一份的高档匿名捐赠品，实际烂极了，都是噱头。
t_en: Death? Not even remotely innovative. I bought it from Dr. Edward. He claimed it was some exclusive fancy-schmancy stuff... But what a disappointment.

t_ch: 效果太差劲，一开始是个长满眼睛的怪物照你肚子来了一刀，之后除了能模模糊糊地看到点高楼和灯光，就只剩下天旋地转了，差点给我晕吐出来……
t_en: The effects were awful. First, some monster covered in eyes stabs you in the gut, and then all you see are blurry glimpses of buildings and lights. The sky was spinning so fast it almost made me puke...

t_ch: …就只有这些？
t_en: Is that all?

t_ch: 不然呢？别太指望匹诺康尼电影业的基本盘…他们还管这种东西叫作「先锋艺术」呢，可笑吧？
t_en: Yeah, what else can you expect? Don't put too much stock in the Penacony movie industry... They even call this junk "groundbreaking art," can you believe it?

t_ch: …说的是啊。不打扰您了，祝您度过愉快的一天。
t_en: ...What a joke. Well, I'll leave you be, then. I hope you have a wonderful day!

t_ch: （长满眼睛的怪物，这描述倒是和那忆域迷因很像，可高楼和灯光…这些东西和「死亡」没什么关系吧。估计只是个用流言蜚语拼凑出来的梦泡吧。）
t_en: (A monster covered in eyes... that sounds like the Memory Zone Meme, but buildings and lights... I don't think those have anything to do with "Death." That whole dream bubble was probably created using rumors and gossip.)

t_ch: 为了让「纯美之歌」继续在银河中传唱，还请您多多支持我的演出。
t_en: Would you be willing to support my performance, and keep the Song of Beauty alive in the cosmos?

t_ch: 这些宝石送给你。
t_en: Here, I got these gems for you.

t_ch: 哇，好漂亮的宝石！不过如此精美的礼物，为什么要随便送给陌生人呢？
t_en: Wow, how fabulous! But why would you give such a wonderful gift to a random stranger like me?

t_ch: 因为我…见不得美梦里有人受穷。
t_en: Well, you see... I can't bear to see anyone in this sweet dream suffering from poverty.

t_ch: 您真是…心地善良。那我就收下了。感谢您，初次见面的先生。如果有机会，也欢迎您来听我唱歌。
t_en: That's... incredibly kind of you. Thank you so much, sir. If you ever get the chance, please feel free to come by and indulge in my singing.

t_ch: 一言为定。不过我还想借此机会请教一下，您可否听说过有关「死亡」的轶事？
t_en: Sure thing. By the way, do you happen to know anything interesting about "Death"?

t_ch: 「死亡」？这…倒是个令人害怕的字眼，和美梦很不相称。
t_en: "Death"? That's... a pretty scary topic, and it doesn't really match the mood of this sweet dream.

t_ch: 实不相瞒，我是个小报记者，正在收集匹诺康尼怪谈。这种东西嘛，想必您也明白…越令人害怕就越容易得到关注。也许您恰好能帮到我？
t_en: You see, I'm a tabloid reporter collecting ghost stories in Penacony. As you know, the more chilling the stories... the more attention they get. Maybe you could help me out?

t_ch: 如果风言风语也可以的话…虽然和死亡无关，但据说在现实酒店，曾经发生过客人一睡不起的情况。
t_en: Well, if you're up for some gossip... It's not about death, but there have been some rumors about a guest at the reality Hotel who fell into a deep sleep and didn't wake up.

t_ch: 似乎是原因不明的昏迷，幸好当事人最后还是恢复意识了…毕竟有家族在保护客人们嘛。
t_en: It was like they were in some sort of coma. Nobody knows what caused it, but luckily, the customer eventually regained consciousness... Well, all customers are under the protection of The Family, after all.

t_ch: 谢谢您，这就够了。愿希佩保佑我们。
t_en: Thank you. This will make for a very juicy headline. May Xipe protect us.

t_ch: （一睡不起？倒是很符合大脑在梦中死亡后，现实身体可能会发生的反应。如果没有醒来那半句就好了。）
t_en: (Unexplained coma? That's actually what happens to your body if your brain dies in a dream, but unfortunately, the customer ended up waking up in the end.)

t_ch: 注意安全，朋友。如果遇到危险，猎犬随时为你效劳。
t_en: Take care, my friend. If you ever find yourself in danger, remember that the Hounds are always ready to help.

t_ch: 这些宝石送给你。
t_en: Here, I got these gems for you.

t_ch: 壮汉神情复杂，仿佛在看着一只沾满泥水的麻雀，不能飞的鸟儿离死也不远了。
t_en: The expression on this hunk of a man was complex, as if he were looking at a mud-soaked sparrow, unable to fly and nearing its end.

t_ch: 朋友…你看起来不太好啊。如果需要帮助，我可以联系酒店帮你强制唤醒。
t_en: You don't look good, my friend... If you need assistance, I can contact the hotel and have them wake you up forcefully.

t_ch: 没必要，我还有事在身…不过，谢了。
t_en: That won't be necessary. I have some business to attend to... but thank you all the same.

t_ch: 行吧。别硬撑着，有需要就来找我们猎犬。
t_en: Alright then. If you ever need help, don't hesitate to reach out to us Hounds.

t_ch: 嗯，我确实想让你帮我一个忙…身为匹诺康尼最优秀的猎犬，你最近有没有和偷渡犯打过交道？
t_en: Well, actually, I do need a favor... As the most outstanding Hound in Penacony, have you come across any stowaways recently?

t_ch: 偷渡犯？匹诺康尼怎么会有偷渡犯呢。从来没有这种事。
t_en: Stowaways? How could there be stowaways in Penacony? We've never had anything like that before.

t_ch: 嗯…好吧。祝你们工作顺利。
t_en: All right... Good luck with your work, then.

t_ch: （家族果然不会告诉公司任何事…唉，简直是废话。）
t_en: (What was I even thinking...? The Family would never share intel with the IPC...)

t_ch: 一醉解千愁，无梦便无忧…嘿，我果然该去当个诗人……
t_en: A sip of liquor, a blissful reprieve... To drown a thousand sorrows, let worries leave... Hah, I know I have what it takes to become a poet...

t_ch: 这些宝石送给你。
t_en: Here, I got these gems for you.

t_ch: 哦？嗝…你要…把这个给我？没想到这地方，居然还有人想当好好先生……
t_en: Oh? You... you are giving... these gems to me? Didn't expect to meet such a generous soul in this place...

t_ch: 还是说，你在可怜我？算了，都无所谓…只要有…苏乐达，就够了。谁让这是梦境的真谛呢？哈哈……
t_en: Or are you just pitying me? Well, it doesn't really matter... As long as I have... SoulGlad... that's enough. This is just a dream after all... Haha...

t_ch: 少喝点苏乐达吧，朋友，对身体有好处。
t_en: You really shouldn't drink so much SoulGlad, my friend. It's not good for your health.

t_ch: 哦！呵呵，你说得对…不过在那之前，我想先见见…苏乐达的恶魔……
t_en: Oh! Hah, maybe I really should quit... but not before meeting... the Devil of SoulGlad...

t_ch: 「苏乐达的恶魔」…能跟我展开讲讲吗？
t_en: The Devil of SoulGlad... Care to elaborate?

t_ch: 呵呵…好…嗐！就是…脖子很大的…海马！
t_en: Haha... yeah... It's a... seahorse... with a long neck!

t_ch: 他们说…它最喜欢出现在醉鬼面前了…尤其是，醉倒在路边的大！笨！蛋！哈，太有趣了……
t_en: They say... it loves to appear to drunk people... especially the ones... who are passed out on the side of the road! Hah! How funny...

t_ch: …确实很有趣。谢谢你。
t_en: ...Yeah, very funny indeed. Thank you.

t_ch: 呃…你是要找我吗？抱歉，我还以为你在看我身后的什么东西。
t_en: Hmm... Were you wanting to talk to me? Sorry, I thought you were checking out something behind me.

t_ch: 这些宝石送给你。
t_en: Here, I got these gems for you.

t_ch: 这是…给我的礼物？确定没有搞错？
t_en: Is this... a gift for me? Are you sure this isn't some kind of mistake?

t_ch: 一点小钱，不成敬意…你就拿着吧。
t_en: Yes, it's for you... Just take it.

t_ch: 这是真的吗？我居然也能得到别人的礼物？不是给我父母，而是给我…谢谢你，真是太谢谢你了！
t_en: Is this for real? Someone is actually giving me a gift? Not for my parents, but for me... Thank you, thank you so much!

t_ch: 朋友，别激动。我只是想向你打听件事。
t_en: Oh, it's not much. I just want to ask you something.

t_ch: …唉，我就知道。你想知道什么？我的父亲，还是母亲？
t_en: ...Ugh, I knew it. What's on your mind? Are you trying to ask about my father, or my mother?

t_ch: 呃…都不是。我只是想请教你，你有没有在梦境里听说过「死亡」？
t_en: Um... neither. I just wanted to know if you've ever heard about "Death" in the Dreamscape?

t_ch: 哦，你这话说的，和我父亲一模一样…他告诉我危险无处不在，即便在梦中也不可掉以轻心。
t_en: Oh, you sound just like my father... Always warning me about danger even in dreams.

t_ch: 他是个智械，入梦方式和有机生命不同。真的出现了危险，没法在梦境中保护我，保护…在这种事上我也远不如他…哈哈，太讽刺了，太讽刺了……
t_en: He's an Intellitron, so his dream entry methods are different from us organics. Can't count on him to protect me if something does go haywire. Funny, right now I'm still under his protection... Heh, how ironic...

t_ch: …朋友，乐观点。是金子总会发光的。
t_en: ...Hey, stay positive. Gold will always shine one day, right?

t_ch: 你想和我聊聊？可以，但不许涉及敏感话题。
t_en: You want to talk to me? Sure, but nothing too sensitive, okay?

t_ch: 这些宝石送给你。
t_en: Here, I got these gems for you.

t_ch: 越是有钱的人，在梦里玩得就越花，但主动给别人送钱的，我还是第一次见。
t_en: Wealthy people have fancy ways to enjoy this dream, but to be honest, I've never seen anyone who gives out money to others like you.

t_ch: 你也要做分发金衣的王子，在烈火中熔化自己的铅心吗？
t_en: So, are you trying to be the prince from the tale, handing out his gold-leaf garment and melting his lead heart in the fire?

t_ch: 哈，你太抬举我了，我怎么会是王子呢。我只是希望这些宝石能打开话匣子，也许你的调查记者生涯恰好和「死亡」有过交集？
t_en: Haha, I'm flattered. I'm no prince, and I just thought these gems would help you speak. So, as an investigative reporter, maybe you have heard of something about "Death"?

t_ch: 哦，又一个好奇的人。你别说，这是我入行时最想做的题材，结果被台长按住了。
t_en: Another curious soul, I see. Well, that was actually the topic I was most into when I entered the industry, but my boss shut it down.

t_ch: 你那位台长是怎么说服你的？
t_en: How did your boss talk you out of it?

t_ch: 理由倒没那么冠冕堂皇…她只说「报道这种没来由的都市传说只会让我们看起来像三流小报」。
t_en: Well... She simply said, "Covering baseless urban legends like that would make us look like some third-rate tabloid."

t_ch: 我想了想，倒也是…诸如在生日许愿时吹蜡烛会被噩梦的幽灵缠上这种事…从我们嘴里说出来确实不太专业了。
t_en: I thought about it, and she had a point... Reporting on stuff like blowing out birthday candles and getting spooked by nightmare ghosts... isn't exactly professional material.

t_ch: 呵，那还真是离奇…不过谢谢你的分享。
t_en: Heh, I guess she's got a point... Thank you for sharing.

t_ch: 还记得我说的吗？你们茨冈尼亚人就适合待在窨井盖下边……
t_en: Remember what I said? You Sigonians are better off hiding in the sewers...

t_ch: 瞧你这鬼鬼祟祟的样子！闻闻这里、嗅嗅那里——
t_en: Look at you, snooping around and sticking your nose everywhere...

t_ch: ——「死亡」的血腥味就那么勾人吗，小孔雀？
t_en: Is the smell of "Death" so enticing, my fine fellow?

t_ch: 哼…是你啊。我早该猜到，知更鸟死后，出现在电视上的「替身」就是你吧，假面愚者。
t_en: Heh... it's you, Masked Fool. I should have guessed it. You're the "imposter" who appeared on TV after Robin's death, right?

t_ch: 听说你被家族下了降头？哎，明明给了你那么直接的提示…「去找个哑巴做朋友」，听听，就这么简单一句话……
t_en: I heard you got caught by The Family? I gave you a clear clue... "Befriend a mute." Simple and straightforward, you know...?

t_ch: 结果呢？你搞砸了不说，还把自己给赔进去了。让你和哑巴交朋友，没让你身先士卒成为哑巴，真是辜负了人家的一片好意啊。
t_en: And what did you do? You messed it up and ended up as their prisoner. I told you to make friends with a mute, not become one yourself. You really let me down.

t_ch: …你什么意思？
t_en: ...What do you mean?

t_ch: 你比我更清楚呀，是谁眼巴巴地看着唱不出歌儿的小鸟横死在面前？当然是你啦，小孔雀～
t_en: You know better than I do. Who watched the little songbird that couldn't sing perish right before their eyes? You did, Blondie.

t_ch: 我是在问你…什么叫「成为哑巴」？
t_en: No, I mean... what did you mean by "becoming one myself"?

t_ch: 明知故问，因为你也快要和她一样，永远说不出话了呗。
t_en: Well, it means you'll soon end up like her, unable to speak ever again.

t_ch: 不过嘛，这在我看来倒不失为好事一桩，因为……
t_en: But it's a good thing, if you ask me, because...

t_ch: 因为我快要触及「真相」了，对么？
t_en: Because I'm getting closer to the truth, right?

t_ch: 愚者，你以为我为什么要拎着这么个破袋子，满大街地分发廉价珠宝？
t_en: Why else do you think I'm handing out cheap trinkets all over the streets, Fool?

t_ch: 这都是做给你看的。我越是狼狈不堪，就越有可能把你钓出来…等你好久了，看在我这么努力的份上，不奖励我一个回答么？
t_en: All part of the act. Fool's bait. The more pathetic I seem, the more likely you'll come sniffing around. So. Now that I've drawn you out, will you reward me with an answer for my efforts?

t_ch: 我有什么理由帮你？
t_en: Why should I help you?

t_ch: 你不是希望匹诺康尼天下大乱么？我能办到，只需求证一件事：那个时候，你让我去找的「哑巴」……
t_en: Don't you want to see Penacony descend into chaos? Well, I can make it happen. I just need an answer to one question: Back then, when you asked me to find a "mute"...

t_ch: …真的是指「知更鸟」么？
t_en: ...Did you really mean Robin?

t_ch: …如果我说「不」呢？
t_en: ...And what if I say "no"?

t_ch: 谢谢。这个字头一回听着这么亲切。
t_en: Then I'll thank you. The word "no" has never sounded so pleasing.

t_ch: 可以啊，是我低估你了。但那又有什么用呢？
t_en: Well done. I admit I underestimated you, but what difference would it make?

t_ch: 告诉你吧——「哑巴」，符合这个定义的人，原先一共有两个。但知更鸟已经死了，而另一个…「她」还在匹诺康尼，但你恐怕再也找不到了。
t_en: Let me tell you something. There were two mutes, but one is dead now, and the other... Though "she" is still in Penacony, I'm afraid you'll never find "her" again.

t_ch: 愚者，现在我能完全确信，我从一开始就走在了正确的方向上，从未偏移。
t_en: Now I'm completely sure that I was on the right track from the beginning and never strayed, Fool.

t_ch: 我手里只缺两样东西了：第一，真相背后的意义；第二，揭露它的方法。
t_en: Right now, there are only two things missing from my grasp: The meaning behind the truth and the means to expose it.

t_ch: 太好了！又到了我最爱的死鸭子嘴硬环节——你这不还是什么都不知道嘛？
t_en: How impressive! That's quite a fancy way of saying "I haven't learned anything so far."

t_ch: 不不，我已经通过种种迹象证明了它确实存在，这就够了。至于这两个问题的答案，十七…不，十六个系统时足够我搞定一切。
t_en: Not exactly. I've gathered enough clues to prove its existence, and that's enough for me. As for the answers to my questions, I'll find them within seventeen... no, sixteen system hours.

t_ch: 十六个系统时啊，真的足够么？那让我再给你添把火吧。
t_en: Oh, really? "Only" sixteen system hours? Well, let me lend you a hand.

t_ch: 喏，给你。这是我珍藏的「相互保证毁灭」按钮，我自己也有个一模一样的。只要我们中有一人按下它，对方就会立刻和整个匹诺康尼一起炸上天。
t_en: Here you go. This is my precious "Mutually Assured Destruction" button, and I have one just like it. When either of us presses it, the other and the whole of Penacony will go up in smoke.

t_ch: 如果你真想要公司入主匹诺康尼，想要到实在受不了…那炸翻牌桌也不失为一种办法。大不了从头来过嘛！公司擅长的就是这个，对吧？
t_en: If you're really so desperate for the IPC to take over Penacony... blowing up the chessboard isn't a bad idea. Start from scratch! That's where the IPC excels, right?

t_ch: 等你走投无路的时候，就按下它吧。当然你也可以联系我，就当是我的「临终关怀」！
t_en: Just press the button when you're at your wit's end. And of course, feel free to reach out to me for my "hospice care" too!

t_ch: 这么危险啊…我猜家族根本没把你的话当真吧？要不你是怎么把它夹带进来的？
t_en: A deadly button, huh?... I guess The Family didn't take your threats seriously at all? Otherwise, how on earth did you manage to bring it in here?

t_ch: 你只要知道我有这个本事就行。
t_en: I have my own ways. That's all you need to know.

t_ch: 恐怕我得拒绝你的提议了，谁知道你这小玩具到底有没有用？顺便，我也不打算去找你口中的「另一位哑巴朋友」，但我很乐于听到这人还在匹诺康尼。
t_en: I'm afraid I'll have to decline your offer. Who knows if your little gadget will actually work? By the way, I have no plans to search for the other mute friend you speak of, but it's good to hear that he's still here in Penacony.

t_ch: 剩下的我自己会办成：我会给家族的垮台准备一场伟大的揭幕表演。等到了最高潮，高墙将崩塌、人们将惊醒，<color=#dbc291ff>不能说话的人也将重新开口</color>——
t_en: I'll handle the rest myself. I'll orchestrate a grand finale for the downfall of The Family. And at the climax, the walls will crumble, people will wake up, and <color=#dbc291ff>those who couldn't speak will find their voices again</color>...

t_ch: ——等到了那个时候，就请你按下按钮，放个大烟花为我助兴吧。回见，愚者。
t_en: When that time comes, go ahead and press the button, and light up the sky with a magnificent fireworks display for me. Catch you later, Fool.

t_ch: 到了这份上还有心思大放厥词…不过，一言为定啦。
t_en: You're still talking big... But sure, if that happens, I'll stay true to my word.

t_ch: 你可千万，别让我失望哦？
t_en: Just don't let me down now, okay?

t_ch: （啊…脑袋里像是有什么东西，是「同谐」开始产生影响了么？）
t_en: (Ehhh... I can feel something inside my head... Is the "Harmony" starting to kick in?)

t_ch: （脑袋里的声音…有点要命啊，越来越近了。）
t_en: (The disturbing voice in my head... it's getting closer.)

t_ch: （每个人加入家族前都要这样受刑么…混蛋，我都想把我的大脑送出去当证据了……）
t_en: (Does everyone have to go through so much torment before joining The Family?... Darn it, now I just want to dig out my brain and use it as evidence...)

t_ch: （苏乐达的恶魔、梦中的危险、噩梦的幽灵…果然在家族承诺的美梦中，「死亡」一定是人们津津乐道的话题。虽然流言众多，但对我有用的线索……）
t_en: (The Devil of SoulGlad, dangers in the dream, and nightmare ghosts... Surely, "Death" is a popular topic in this sweet dream granted by The Family. I've collected a bunch of rumors, but no useful clues...)

t_ch: （不知不觉间，袋子要见底了啊…看看最后一位幸运儿，能不能再给我个惊喜吧。）
t_en: (The gems in my bag are running low... Let's see if my last lucky interviewee brings some surprises.)

t_ch: 加拉赫，什么风把你给吹来了？
t_en: What brings you here, Gallagher?

t_ch: 几位老朋友找上门。现在有空吗，舒翁？
t_en: Some friends from the old days. Do you have a moment to spare, Siobhan?

t_ch: 我都空了一天啦。各位，欢迎光临「惊梦酒吧」——
t_en: I have the whole day to spare. Ladies and gentlemen, welcome to Dreamjolt Hostelry!

t_ch: 酒水单上的什么都卖，唯独苏乐达不卖，这里没有那种无聊的饮料；酒吧里的什么都不卖，唯独快乐能购买，这里只愿你能够开怀大笑。
t_en: This bar offers a wide variety of drinks, but we draw the line at SoulGlad. Why settle for ordinary when you can experience extraordinary? We're dedicated to serving up nothing but pure joy and laughter.

t_ch: 想喝点什么？我来准备。
t_en: What would you like to drink? I'll whip it up for you.

t_ch: 快看，{NICKNAME}，是个好帅气的大姐姐——和希露瓦同一款哎！
t_en: Look, {NICKNAME}! A lady as cool as Serval!

t_ch: 确实帅。
t_en: She's cool, indeed.

t_ch: 就是本人。
t_en: I think she is Serval.

t_ch: 希露瓦是谁？介绍给我认识认识呗。
t_en: Who's Serval? Will you introduce me to her?

t_ch: 呀，被人家听到啦……
t_en: Oops, she's heard us...

t_ch: 别逗他们了，我的好酒保。不劳烦你，今天我来露一手。
t_en: Just spare them, my esteemed bartender. I'll take over the bar today.

t_ch: 年纪大了，再不复健，怕是连当年吃饭的手艺都要忘咯。调饮用的原材料呢，你放哪儿了？
t_en: I'm getting up there in age, and I need some practice before I forget the skills that used to put food on my table. Where did you stash the ingredients?

t_ch: 喏，就在柜台下面。几位客人远道而来，你不打算弄点特调饮品么？
t_en: They're all under the counter. Since our guests have traveled from afar, shouldn't you whip up some special drinks?

t_ch: 正有此意。朋友们，帮我做件事——<color=#dbc291ff>在酒吧里四处转转，把喜欢的材料带回来吧</color>。
t_en: That's exactly what I had in mind. Hey, my friends, do me a favor. <color=#dbc291ff>Go around the bar and bring me any ingredients you fancy.</color>

t_ch: 这案子估计得谈上很久，我来为各位准备些合适的饮品——量身定制，不含酒精。
t_en: The discussion might take quite some time, so I'll prepare some customized, non-alcoholic drinks for you.

t_ch: 酒吧里？能用的材料不都在柜台上吗？
t_en: In the bar? But aren't all the ingredients right there on the counter?

t_ch: 这里是梦境，可爱的小姐，只要你想，任何东西都可以下咽，安逸、饥饿、迷茫、厌倦…应有尽有，俯拾皆是。
t_en: Why, we're in a dream, my lovely lady. You can help yourself to anything if you wish for it — comfort, hunger, confusion, or even boredom... It's all within reach, right at your fingertips.

t_ch: 她管我叫可爱的小姐哎…！
t_en: Did you hear that! She just called me "my lovely lady"...!

t_ch: 即便在现实中，调饮也不只是酒水和食材的混合，调饮师会捕捉吧台上的氛围，注重手法与故事的结合，再加入一点小小的神秘和期待……
t_en: Even in reality, mixing drinks is more than just throwing ingredients together. A bartender needs to capture the bar's atmosphere, master technique, and spin a tale of mystery and anticipation...

t_ch: 才能和顾客一同调制出每个人人生的风味。
t_en: Only then can a perfect drink, crafted with the customer's life story, be created.

t_ch: 换句话说——喝到什么都看命。所以别想太多，犹豫不决是享受的大忌。
t_en: In other words, what you get from your drink is down to luck. So don't overthink it. Indecisiveness has no place when it comes to enjoyment.

t_ch: 说了这么多，其实调饮远没有你想的那么复杂，挑选喜欢的材料，再全部丢进杯子里拌匀，就这么简单。
t_en: That being said, mixing a drink is way simpler than you'd imagine. Just pick your favorite ingredients, toss them in a glass, mix it up, and it's done.

t_ch: 在酒吧里四处转转，把喜欢的材料带回来，决定好了就跟我说吧。
t_en: So go ahead, explore the bar and bring me any ingredients you prefer.

t_ch: 我找齐想要的材料了。
t_en: I've gathered all the ingredients I need.

t_ch: 哼，很麻利嘛。让我看看……
t_en: Nice work. Let me take a look...

t_ch: 那我再去找找。
t_en: I'll come back later.

t_ch: 去吧，朋友。相信你会有更多收获。
t_en: Go ahead, my friend. I'm sure you'll find splendid ingredients.

t_ch: 别小看匹诺康尼的调饮师，尤其是加拉赫。这家伙八成会点魔法…无论顾客的需求多么刁钻，他都能给你调出恰到好处的滋味。
t_en: And let me tell you, don't underestimate the bartenders of Penacony, especially not Gallagher. This guy is pure magic... He can satisfy even the pickiest of customers.

t_ch: 机会难得，你们可要给这个老家伙出点难题啊。
t_en: Don't go easy on this old man. Give him a real challenge.

t_ch: 你看，这瓶子里的液体颜色还怪漂亮的。标签上写着…「美梦糖浆•超级浓厚」。好像没找到保质期，封装日期写的是……
t_en: Check out this bottle. The liquid inside looks beautiful, and the label reads... Dream Syrup (Thick). I don't see an expiration date, but the production date is...

t_ch: <size=36>…半个琥珀纪前？！</size>
t_en: <size=36>...Half an Amber Era ago?</size>

t_ch: 这个要是给人喝了肯定会出事的吧……
t_en: Drinking this stuff can't lead to anything good. That's for sure...

t_ch: 说不定还没过期。
t_en: Maybe it hasn't expired yet.

t_ch: 噫…这玩意都结块了，摇都摇不动…就算没过期我也绝对不想喝这个。
t_en: Ugh... the syrup has turned clumpy... I definitely don't want to drink this even if it's not expired.

t_ch: 不是说梦里什么都能喝嘛？
t_en: But I thought we could drink anything in dreams?

t_ch: 话是这么说没错，可是…这个也未免太……
t_en: That's true, but this... really doesn't seem fresh...

t_ch: 你们看上我珍藏的那瓶糖浆了？放心，这是在梦里，不会闹肚子的。陈放了好久，味道应该相当不错。拿去吧，招待你们这样可爱的客人正好。
t_en: Did you find the bottle of syrup I've been hoarding? Don't worry, it's all just a dream, and it won't upset your stomach. It's been aging for years, and should have a refined taste by now. Feel free to have some. It's perfect for entertaining lovely guests like you.

t_ch: 那…那我们就先收着吧。
t_en: Then... we'll keep it for now...

t_ch: 是苏乐达！那姐姐不是说店里不卖这个嘛？
t_en: It's SoulGlad! But I thought they didn't sell SoulGlad at the bar?

t_ch: ——我摇！嗯，气还很足，保质期也很安全！
t_en: Let's give it a shake! Nice, it's still fizzy and has a long shelf life!

t_ch: 或许是其他人带过来的。
t_en: Maybe someone else brought it here.

t_ch: 有可能。这东西随处可见，有人随手拿进来一瓶也不稀奇。
t_en: That's possible. You see SoulGlad everywhere these days, so it wouldn't be a surprise if someone brought a bottle here.

t_ch: 或许是摆在这做装饰的。
t_en: Maybe it's placed here for decoration.

t_ch: 也许吧。匹诺康尼的经典饮品，酒吧里少了它确实有点怪怪的。
t_en: Yeah, perhaps. SoulGlad is a classic in Penacony, and it'd be strange for a bar not to have it.

t_ch: 别，你摇过我们就用不了了。
t_en: Don't! It's no good for our drink if all shaken up.

t_ch: 啊！我…没、没事，多放一会儿就好了…吧。
t_en: Uh-huh! I... Let's just leave it here for a while... and it'll be alright, right?

t_ch: 为什么这间酒吧不卖苏乐达呢，是有什么隐情吗？
t_en: Why don't they sell SoulGlad in this bar? Did something happen?

t_ch: 是调饮师的矜持吧。如果来这的客人还能点到外面随处可见的饮料，就太没意思了。我在冲咖啡时也会有这样的想法。
t_en: It's all about the bartender's pride. It wouldn't make sense for customers to come here and order drinks they can find anywhere else. That's the mindset I use when I brew my coffee.

t_ch: 呃…哈哈，姬子姐姐说得对。
t_en: Um... Haha, you're right, Himeko.

t_ch: 这个能用来调饮吗？咦，下面还压着张纸条…「需以珍视之物换取」。
t_en: Can this be used for mixing drinks? Oh, and there's a note underneath... "Exchange with your precious things."

t_ch: 是要我们拿东西交换？可是应该换什么呢…又要交给谁？
t_en: What should we exchange for it?... And who should we give our stuff to?

t_ch: 投入「崇高道德的赞许」…
t_en: Give "Praise of High Morals"...

t_ch: 你以某种形而上的方式将「崇高道德的赞许」放入了空杯里……
t_en: You put "Praise of High Morals" into the empty cup in a metaphysical manner...

t_ch: …哇，纸条上的字变了！
t_en: ...Whoa, the words on the note are changing!

t_ch: 「幸福理应属于高尚之人」…这话的意思是我们可以把它拿走咯？嘿嘿，平时多做好事还是会有回报的嘛。多谢啦！
t_en: "Happiness belongs to the noble." Does this mean we can take it? Ahaha, doing good deeds does pay off! Thank you!

t_ch: 投入「<unbreak>50000</unbreak>信用点」…
t_en: Give <unbreak>50,000</unbreak> credits...

t_ch: 你将「<unbreak>50000</unbreak>信用点」放入了空杯里……
t_en: You put <unbreak>50,000</unbreak> credits into the empty cup

t_ch: …哇，纸条上的字变了！
t_en: ...Whoa, the words on the note are changing!

t_ch: 「钱币铸成的阶梯亦可通往幸福」…这话的意思是我们可以把它拿走咯？虽然不知道划不划算…不过还是多谢啦！
t_en: "Stairs made of coins can lead to happiness." Does this mean we can take it? I'm not sure if it's a good deal... but thank you anyway!

t_ch: 投入「一袋垃圾」…
t_en: Give a bag of trash...

t_ch: 你将「一袋垃圾」放入了空杯里……
t_en: You put a bag of trash into the empty cup

t_ch: 你干嘛呀！垃圾不能随便…不会吧？！纸条上的字居然变了！
t_en: Hey, what are you doing! You can't just throw trash... No way! The words on the note are changing!

t_ch: 「幸福是卑微者的梦」…这句话的意思是我们可以把它拿走咯？总觉得哪里怪怪的……
t_en: "Happiness is the dream of the humble." Does this mean we can take it? Something feels weird about this...

t_ch: 似乎没有可以投入的东西…
t_en: There doesn't seem to be anything that can be put in...

t_ch: 这里洒满了筹码哎……
t_en: Hey, look at all these chips scattered everywhere...

t_ch: 前几天有个鸢尾花家系的演员来砸场子，和舒翁起了点争执…估计是那时候落下的。
t_en: A few days ago, an actor from the Iris Family came — caused a ruckus with Siobhan... Those chips must have gotten scattered during all the chaos.

t_ch: 能想象场面有多激烈。
t_en: I can only imagine how intense that scene must have been.

t_ch: 嗐，单方面碾压罢了。
t_en: Nah, it turned out to be a landslide victory.

t_ch: 梦里还能有人爆金币？
t_en: So, enemies drop gold coins even in a dream?

t_ch: 哈哈，这个比喻巧妙。
t_en: Haha, that's a clever metaphor.

t_ch: 这么说来，能把酒吧开在这片…到处都是怪物的地方，也挺不可思议的。舒翁姐姐肯定是个很厉害的人吧？
t_en: That being said, opening a bar in this place... filled with monsters... is quite a feat. Siobhan must have a lot of tricks up her sleeve, right?

t_ch: 这你就得亲自去问她了。不过我猜她不会松口的，除非你能调出一杯让她心服口服的饮料。
t_en: You'll have to ask her yourself to find out. But I have a feeling she won't budge unless you impress her with an incredible drink.

t_ch: …我前面说得还不够清楚吗，艾米绮小姐？「惊梦酒吧」只欢迎想尽情享受饮品的客人。抱歉…我对你的提案没兴趣。
t_en: Haven't I made myself clear enough, Ms. Amiky? The Dreamjolt Hostelry only welcomes guests who want to enjoy a drink to their heart's content. I'm sorry, but I'm not interested in your proposal.

t_ch: 可你明明有那份才能！你一定能吸引所有人的目光…你天生就属于鸢尾花的舞台，而不是这个破木台子！
t_en: But you have the talent! You'll attract a huge audience... You're destined for the Iris' stage, not for this run-down shack!

t_ch: 跟我走吧！我们一定能成为匹诺康尼最瞩目的明星，把光芒洒进梦境的所有角落！拜托了，舒翁，我需要你……
t_en: Come with me! We'll become the talk of Penacony, shining light into every corner of the Dreamscape! Please, Siobhan, I really need you...

t_ch: …如你所见，我正在招待客人。别让我重复第二遍。
t_en: ...As you see, I'm entertaining my guests. Don't make me repeat myself.

t_ch: 好…如果你不答应，我就在这坐着，哪都不去。给我一杯带气泡的饮料。甜的，多加冰。
t_en: Fine... If you don't come along, I'll just sit here and not go anywhere else. Give me a sparkling drink, sweet, with extra ice.

t_ch: 稍等。
t_en: Just one moment.

t_ch: 怎么回事…这人要一直占着吧台，咱们可没法聊案子了。
t_en: What's her deal...? We can't discuss the case with other people hanging around the bar.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，你先前施展过的「钟表把戏」…还能再试一次吗？
t_en: {NICKNAME}, can you do that clockwork trick of yours again...?

t_ch: 就等这句话呢。
t_en: Just what I've been waiting for.

t_ch: 怎么就连姬子也…
t_en: How come even you, Himeko...

t_ch: 时间有限，我们得抓紧了。
t_en: Time is running out. We need to hurry.

t_ch: 是啊，拜托你了。
t_en: Yeah, I'm counting on you.

t_ch: 你是舒翁的客人吧，有什么事？如果想劝我走就免了，除非她答应我的请求……
t_en: You're one of Siobhan's guests, right? What can I do for you? If you're here to convince me to leave, please stop it. I'll never leave until she accepts my proposal...

t_ch: 到底为什么呢？为什么她就是不肯离开这里…这个冷清的破烂酒吧？
t_en: I just don't get why she won't leave this place... this run-down shack with no customers whatsoever.

t_ch: 尝试洞察她的内心。
t_en: Try reading her thoughts.

t_ch: 我也不太清楚原因…
t_en: I've no idea either...

t_ch: 没关系。我会一直坐着，直到她改变心意为止。
t_en: Ah, forget it. I'll just stay here and wait until she changes her mind.

t_ch: 舒翁刚才说过「任何东西都可以下咽」…也许你可以进行各种尝试，看看<color=#dbc291ff>不同情绪能酿出何种不同的调饮原料</color>。
t_en: Siobhan just said, "Anything can be imbibed"... Perhaps you can try various experiments to see what<color=#dbc291ff> different emotions can brew with different drink ingredients</color>.

t_ch: 我看到了…我和舒翁共同登上舞台的时刻。
t_en: I've seen it... the moment when Siobhan and I share the stage.

t_ch: 观众们在鼓掌，欢呼声如潮水般涌来，悠扬的旋律奏响，彩带挂满肩头，空气弥漫着鸢尾花的芬芳，这滋味…比蜜糖还要甜。
t_en: The crowd is going wild, applause crashing like waves. The aroma of irises fills the air, a beautiful melody playing, ribbons dancing around us. And the taste... is sweeter than honey.

t_ch: 我已见过这景象无数次，虽然是在梦里，但每一回都令我心醉神迷。也正因如此，无论如何我都要把她带回那个世界。
t_en: I've seen that scene countless times in my dreams, and every time, it mesmerizes me. That's why I have to bring her back to that world, no matter what it takes.

t_ch: 要来碰杯吗？善于倾听的陌生宾客，就当是…为这个遥不可及的梦提前庆祝一番。
t_en: Want to raise a glass, my attentive listener? Let's consider it... a toast to my far-fetched dream.

t_ch: 艾米绮将噼咔白葡萄汽水注满你们的高脚杯。杯沿轻轻相碰，一声脆响传来。
t_en: Amiky poured Pika White Grape Soda into your tall glasses. The rims gently clinked, emitting a crisp, resonant sound.

t_ch: （她还是不肯走…或许得再尝试一下。）
t_en: (She's still not leaving... Maybe I'll have to try again.)

t_ch: …很可笑吧？明明清楚我们的人生只是两条平行线，可我却依然紧抓着她不放……
t_en: ...It's ridiculous, right? Our paths were never meant to cross, yet I'm still holding on to her...

t_ch: 我胆小、怯懦，向往发光的地方，却不敢走到聚光灯下。我需要她的指引，因为我自己什么都做不到……
t_en: I'm too timid and shy, longing to shine but afraid of stepping into the spotlight. I need her guidance, because I'll never be able to do anything alone...

t_ch: 你不了解舒翁的过去，不知道她是个…多么闪耀的人。即便在巨星云集的鸢尾花家系，她的才华也无可追及。
t_en: You don't know Siobhan's past, and you have no clue how... radiant she used to be. Even among the talented Iris Family, her skill was unmatched.

t_ch: 我知道…舒翁肯定觉得我是想借她的名声出人头地。可我想要的，只是她能够回到那里……
t_en: I know... she probably thinks I'm just trying to ride her fame to get ahead, but all I want is for her to reclaim her place...

t_ch: 你感到一股莫名的苦涩和甘甜交织，弥散在空气中。下一秒，那情绪化作液体注满了你的高脚杯。
t_en: You feel an inexplicable mix of bitterness and sweetness that permeates the air. The next moment, that emotion turns into liquid filling your goblet.

t_ch: （她还是不肯走…或许得再尝试一下。）
t_en: (She's still not leaving... Maybe I'll have to try again.)

t_ch: ——都是那帮*混蛋鸢尾花*害的！是他们逼的舒翁无路可走，只能躲在这里，经营一间怪物酒吧…都是算计和阴谋！
t_en: Those darn Iris jerks! They're the ones who forced Siobhan into hiding here, running this pesky bar... It's all their dirty scheming!

t_ch: 啊…我明白了，她不是不愿离开，只是不想再面对那群小人！
t_en: Ah... I get it now. She's not leaving because she doesn't want to run into them again!

t_ch: 我…我可以帮她把路铺平——我能帮上她的忙！我回去就给梦主写信，控诉鸢尾花家系的罪行…舒翁肯定会感谢我的！
t_en: I... I can help clear the way for her. I can do her a favor! I'll go back and write a letter to the Dreammaster, exposing the crimes committed by the Iris Family... Siobhan will definitely appreciate it!

t_ch: （再让她发散下去就大事不妙了…再想想办法吧。）
t_en: (Things might get out of control if she gets any angrier... I need to come up with another plan.)

t_ch: 哎…和你聊过后，总感觉心里不是滋味。各种念想混在一起，有点痛快，又有点想哭……
t_en: Well... talking to you has got me feeling a bit down. My thoughts are swirling, making my mind clear and bringing tears to my eyes...

t_ch: 或许…我是该找个地方重新想想，对我来说，舒翁究竟意味着什么……
t_en: Maybe... I should find a place to reflect on what Siobhan truly means to me...

t_ch: 这是饮料钱，请帮我转交给她。我先走了，再见。
t_en: Here's the payment for the drinks. Please pass it on to her. I'm leaving now.

t_ch: …艾米绮走了？
t_en: ...Amiky has left?

t_ch: 这样对她也好…盛会的梦固然迷人，可梦就是梦，不是现实。她那杯饮料我请了，钱你们收着吧。
t_en: *sigh* That's good for her... Radiant dreams may be enticing, but they're nothing more than dreams. Her drink is on the house. Please keep the money.

t_ch: 准备好了就去找加拉赫——我看他已经手痒难耐啦。
t_en: When you're ready, go to Gallagher. I can tell he's itching to show off his skills.

t_ch: …找来了很有趣的东西啊，挑挑吧。
t_en: ...You've found some interesting ingredients there. Now, take your pick.

t_ch: 每种饮料的风味各不相同，基底的味道通常决定了第一印象和回味的余韵——<color=#dbc291ff>你想选择哪种材料作为基底</color>？
t_en: Each drink has its own unique flavor, and the base ingredient sets the tone for the initial taste and the lingering aftertaste. So, <color=#dbc291ff>which one would you like to use as the base</color>?

t_ch: 哼，整个匹诺康尼都找不出第二种比它更甜腻的饮料，但如今的逐梦客好的就是这一口。
t_en: Heh, you won't find a sweeter drink anywhere in Penacony, and that's what today's dream seekers crave.

t_ch: 比苏萨水更甜，比美梦糖浆更苦。{RUBY_B#苏乐达}「瓶中美梦」{RUBY_E#}——匹诺康尼的形象代言。
t_en: Sweeter than Sousa Juice and more bitter than Dream Syrup. {RUBY_B#Soulglad}"Dream in a Bottle"{RUBY_E#} — that's a metaphor for Penacony itself.

t_ch: 据说这是那位哈努努最爱的饮料，当然，说的是它的原型。这玩意又酸又苦，气泡在喉咙里炸开，像咽了带锈的铁链，让人联系到监狱和牢房。
t_en: The original formula is said to be Hanunue's favorite drink. It's sour and bitter, and the bubbles burst in your throat like swallowing rusty chains, evoking thoughts of dungeons and prisons.

t_ch: 基底选好了，接下来轮到辅料，它负责与基底产生奇妙的化学反应，不能喧宾夺主，又必须带来难忘的味觉体验，<color=#dbc291ff>你想选择哪种材料作为辅料</color>？
t_en: Now that you've chosen the base, it's time to pick the adjunct — the ingredient that'll create a marvelous chemical reaction with the base. It should give an unforgettable taste without overpowering the main tone. So, <color=#dbc291ff>what's your choice for the adjunct</color>?

t_ch: 美好未来…这滋味真是久违了。
t_en: Bright Future... a taste that's been a long time coming.

t_ch: 我永远忘不了，当米哈伊尔带着我在梦境的荒野中策马飞驰时，忆质的流风中就裹着这种味道。那段时光令人魂牵梦萦…美得太不真实。
t_en: I'll never forget the flavor that danced on my tongue as Mikhail whisked me through the Dreamscape wilderness. Those were haunting times... too beautiful to be real.

t_ch: 这种滋味…不算热烈，但回味无穷。
t_en: The taste is... not very intense, yet evocative.

t_ch: 在那群衣冠禽兽送走米哈伊尔后，我在梦中的荒野风餐露宿。有人说，美梦连泥土都渗满了糖浆…呵，可我尝到的只有坚忍的苦涩。
t_en: After those despicables sent Mikhail away, I found myself lost in the wilderness of my dreams. They say that even the dirt here oozes with sweetness... Heh, all I tasted was stoic bitterness.

t_ch: 这是口感最猛烈的辅料…我的最爱。
t_en: This is the most pungent adjunct we have... and my personal favorite.

t_ch: 米哈伊尔临走前，他眼角的皱纹已经太深，如刀割般吓人。他嗫嚅着，气息弱到攀不上喉头，只能郁积在胸膛。但我能嗅到…那一晚的匹诺康尼散发着这种味道。
t_en: Before Mikhail left, the wrinkles at the corners of his eyes had deepened, like knife cuts. He mumbled, his breath too weak to climb up his throat, caught up in his chest. But I could smell it... the lingering scent of Penacony itself on that night.

t_ch: 米哈伊尔…？
t_en: Mikhail...?

t_ch: 差不多了。来选个装饰吧，你喜欢什么风格？我这里都有。
t_en: Almost there. Let's pick a decoration. Which style do you prefer? Anything you need, I've got it.

t_ch: 同心圆吸管。
t_en: Straws with concentric circles.

t_ch: {RUBY_B#同谐}同心圆{RUBY_E#}…愿你不会忘记自己的初心。
t_en: {RUBY_B#The Harmony}Concentric circles{RUBY_E#}... May you always remember where you started.

t_ch: 哈努兄弟立牌。
t_en: Hanu's sign.

t_ch: 勇敢无畏的{RUBY_B#哈努努}反抗者{RUBY_E#}，祝你也能像他一样咬碎镣铐。
t_en: A {RUBY_B#Hanunue}symbol{RUBY_E#} of bravery and dauntlessness. May you break free from your shackles just like he did.

t_ch: 金币柠檬片。
t_en: Coin-shaped lemon slices.

t_ch: {RUBY_B#星际和平公司}寰宇企业{RUBY_E#}的最爱…你很有野心。
t_en: The IPC's favorite... Ambitious, aren't you?

t_ch: 好了，大功告成……
t_en: Well, it's done...

t_ch: 用这杯「黄金国」向你致意，逐梦客——
t_en: Here's to you, dream seeker, with this glass of El Dorado...

t_ch: ——敬金色的美梦。
t_en: ...To golden dreams.

t_ch: 用这杯「过早的埋葬」向你致意，梦想家——
t_en: Here's to you, dreamer, with this glass of "The Premature Burial"...

t_ch: ——敬停滞的时间。
t_en: ...To bringing time to a standstill.

t_ch: 用这杯「梦中之梦」向你致意，清醒之人——
t_en: Here's to you, sober one, with this glass of "a Dream Within a Dream"...

t_ch: ——敬自掘坟墓的父辈。
t_en: ...To our forebearers who dug their own graves.

t_ch: 用这杯「长眠不醒」向你致意，迷失的羔羊——
t_en: Here's to you, lost sheep, with this glass of "The Big Sleep"...

t_ch: ——敬往日的幽灵。
t_en: ...To the ghosts of the past.

t_ch: 用这杯「漫长的告别」向你致意，守望者——
t_en: Here's to you, watcher, with this glass of "The Long Goodbye"...

t_ch: ——敬苦涩的童年。
t_en: ...To a bitter childhood.

t_ch: 用这杯「再见，吾爱」向你致意，寻索真相者——
t_en: Here's to you, seeker of truth, with this glass of "Farewell, My Lovely"...

t_ch: ——敬未竟的心愿。
t_en: ...To unfinished business.

t_ch: 用这杯「到坟场的车票」向你致意，无名客——
t_en: Here's to you, the Nameless, with this glass of "A Ticket to the Boneyard"...

t_ch: ——敬已死的和将死的人。
t_en: ...To the dead and the dying.

t_ch: 用这杯「向邪恶追索」向你致意，开拓者——
t_en: Here's to you, Trailblazer, with this glass of "Even the Wicked"...

t_ch: ——敬悲哀的真相。
t_en: ...To the sad truth.

t_ch: 用这杯「谋杀与创造之时」向你致意，变革者——
t_en: Here's to you, innovator, with this glass of "Time to Murder and Create"...

t_ch: ——敬伟大的复仇。
t_en: ...To great vengeance.

t_ch: 可以啊，加拉赫，宝刀未老。
t_en: Well done, Gallagher. You're not over the hill yet.

t_ch: 各位呢，可还满意？
t_en: So, are you satisfied?

t_ch: 这味道…比苏乐达复杂得多哎。
t_en: The flavors... they're way more sophisticated than SoulGlad.

t_ch: 口感丰富、层次分明，真是杰作。尤其是辅料的处理，我能尝到某种别样的风味，辛辣、酸涩，却又带一点甘甜……
t_en: The richness and layers of these flavors are a masterpiece, especially with the adjuncts. I can taste the spicy and sour notes with a hint of sweetness...

t_ch: 我不知道这意味着什么，也许加拉赫先生愿意讲解一下自己的巧思？
t_en: I'm not entirely sure what it all means. Maybe Mr. Gallagher can shed some light on it?

t_ch: 很可惜，如果你在期待一个深刻的回答，恐怕要失望了。
t_en: Well, if you're expecting a profound answer, I'm afraid I'll disappoint you.

t_ch: 它所蕴含的意象非常简单…这不过是美梦乐园真正的滋味，仅此而已。
t_en: The imagery it implies is pretty straightforward... It's just a glimpse of what this dream truly tastes like. Nothing more.

t_ch: 这真正的滋味…和那位<color=#dbc291ff>米哈伊尔</color>有关吗？
t_en: Does this "true taste" have anything to do with that <color=#dbc291ff>Mikhail</color>?

t_ch: 米哈伊尔…
t_en: Mikhail...

t_ch: 是梦里出现过的名字…
t_en: I heard this name in my dream...

t_ch: 是啊，我就说在哪听过…{NICKNAME}被那个假面愚者姑娘迷晕的时候，好像听见过有人在念叨这个名字，对不对？
t_en: Yeah, that name does sound familiar... When you got knocked out by that Masked Fool girl, {NICKNAME}, I think I heard someone calling that name. Do you remember?

t_ch: 呵…知道的不少啊，果然没看错你们。这下我也没理由不向各位坦诚了。
t_en: Heh... I was right about you. You guys seem to know quite a bit, and now there's no reason to hide anything from you anymore.

t_ch: 那就展开讲讲案子吧，当然…也会附赠那位<color=#dbc291ff>米哈伊尔</color>的故事。
t_en: Let's dig deeper into the case, and of course... I'll tell you a story about <color=#dbc291ff>Mikhail</color>.

t_ch: 先说结论吧：根据家族手上的线索，这位流萤确实不是本地人，也不是受邀前来的宾客。换言之…她是个货真价实的偷渡犯。
t_en: Alright, let's start with what we know based on the clues The Family has. It seems that Firefly isn't a local or an invited guest. In other words... she's a stowaway.

t_ch: 我也被这姑娘骗了，当真是年纪大咯。不过在盛会之星，偷渡不是多么稀罕的事，也不难查。事发后猎犬们立即采取了行动，从梦境和现实两头开始追踪。
t_en: She managed to fool me at first — my age must be getting the best of me. But here, on the Planet of Festivities, stowaways are a common sight. You're bound to run into one sooner or later. After the incident, the Hounds wasted no time searching for that girl in both the Dreamscape and reality.

t_ch: 可结果…只有一个坏消息，也是最让人头疼的消息。
t_en: But here's the thing... We only received bad news, and the trickiest kind at that.

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>这小姑娘人间蒸发了</color>，梦里没留下任何痕迹，现实中也完全找不到身体，仿佛从没来过匹诺康尼。
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>She simply vanished</color>, leaving no trace in the Dreamscape or reality, as if she had never come to Penacony at all.

t_ch: 啊？这岂不是意味着……
t_en: Huh? Does that mean...

t_ch: 「死亡」完全消灭了她…
t_en: "Death" eradicated her...

t_ch: 不可能。现在的问题不是*她死了*，而是*她仿佛从没出现过*。
t_en: That's impossible. The problem now is not that she's dead, but that it's as if she had never existed in the first place.

t_ch: 事情或许还有转机？
t_en: Maybe things will turn out different than what we think?

t_ch: 转机…你想说那姑娘没有真的遇害？劝你清醒点，这案子的目击证人不就是你自己么？
t_en: Different...? Are you suggesting that girl wasn't actually killed? Come on, aren't you a witness in this case?

t_ch: 她是个幽灵？！
t_en: Is she a ghost!?

t_ch: 哈哈，这个比喻不错…呃，这是个比喻吧？
t_en: Haha, a clever metaphor... Um, you were making a metaphor, right?

t_ch: 我就直截了当地说了，这姑娘的情况…别说你们，猎犬家系都是头一回见。
t_en: Let me be frank... This case, actually... is unlike anything the Bloodhound Family has dealt with before.

t_ch: 「头一回」…所以在匹诺康尼，「死亡」确有其事，是么？
t_en: "Dealt with before"? So... "Death" does happen in Penacony, if I understand correctly?

t_ch: 都被你们看见了，还有什么可瞒的。一座城市有光鲜亮丽的表面，就肯定有不可告人的背面，成年人的事不用我多说。
t_en: You've witnessed it, so there's no need to hide. Even the shiniest city has its dark side. We're all adults here, surely I don't need to explain too much to you.

t_ch: 如果仅凭这点就想质疑家族，未免太天真了。美梦中也有意外死亡，那又怎样？这种极小概率事件…会影响的也就极少数人。
t_en: Confronting The Family based on that alone would be naive. Death may occur in sweet dreams — so what? Such events are highly unlikely and only affect a tiny number of people.

t_ch: 要是你们真想深入这起案子，就得先搞明白家族真正的「难处」。
t_en: If you really want to delve deeper into this case, you need to understand the true "problem" with The Family.

t_ch: 我猜，现在该说到那位「米哈伊尔」的故事了吧。
t_en: I guess it's time to tell the story of that "Mikhail."

t_ch: 你很敏锐。星穹列车也收到了那只八音盒，对不？知道里面藏着什么秘密么？
t_en: You're very perceptive. The Astral Express has received that music box, too, right? Do you know the secrets it holds?

t_ch: 一句留言：「将梦中的不可能之事尽收眼底，寻得匹诺康尼之父『钟表匠』的遗产，而后解答：生命因何而沉睡。」
t_en: There's a message. "...Witness the impossible in the realm of dreams, find the Legacy of the Watchmaker, Father of Penacony, and thus the answer to the question: Why does life slumber?"

t_ch: 一字不差。
t_en: That's the exact wording.

t_ch: 哎，你笑什么…难道是你写的？文采还挺好。
t_en: Hey, why are you laughing... Wait, did you write it? It's quite poetic.

t_ch: 我是负责查案的治安官，怎么可能不知道…我猜你们一定也察觉到这句留言并非出自家族之手了——甚至两者的关系没那么好这件事？
t_en: No... but I'm the officer in charge of this case, and how could I not know?... I'm sure you must have noticed that this message didn't come from The Family. You might even have guessed that the relationship between The Family and the Watchmaker isn't as close as it seems?

t_ch: 目前还只是推测，我们很难相信{RUBY_B#「钟表匠」}匹诺康尼之父{RUBY_E#}和{RUBY_B#家族}它的实际管理人{RUBY_E#}这么不对付。
t_en: That's just our speculation. Actually, it's hard to believe that the {RUBY_B#The Watchmaker}Father of Penacony{RUBY_E#} and its {RUBY_B#The Family}actual managers{RUBY_E#} are at odds.

t_ch: 现在我可以告诉你，完全正确。
t_en: Now I can assure you that your speculation is absolutely correct.
